structured_payload_candidate.title: Head Pressure Control – Fixed Speed CW Pumps
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Water-cooled chiller plant with fixed speed CW pumps
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Map head pressure control loop output: 0-50% resets maximum cooling tower speed from 100% to minimum; 50-100% resets head pressure control valve position from 100% open to MinCWVlvPos.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Head pressure control loop is enabled for an operating chiller.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Map loop output as described to limit tower speed and modulate CW isolation valve to maintain minimum lift.
configurable_values: MinCWVlvPos (minimum head pressure control valve position)
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: For each chiller, map loop output as follows: a. From 0-50%, the loop output shall reset maximum cooling tower speed point, HpTowerMaxSpd, from 100% to minimum speed. b. From 50-100%, the loop output shall reset head pressure control valve position from 100% open to MinCWVlvPos.

evidence_quote: For each chiller, map loop output as follows: a. From 0-50%, the loop output shall reset maximum cooling tower speed point, HpTowerMaxSpd, from 100% to minimum speed. b. From 50-100%, the loop output shall reset head pressure control valve position from 100% open to MinCWVlvPos.

structured_payload_candidate.title: VAV AHU – FC#6: OA Fraction Error
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Multiple-zone VAV AHU

structured_payload_candidate.title: Zone Temperature Alarms
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: All zones
structured_payload_candidate.summary: High-temperature alarm: Level 4 if zone is 2°C above cooling setpoint for 10 min; Level 3 if 3°C above for 10 min. Low-temperature alarm: Level 4 if zone is 2°C below heating setpoint for 10 min; Level 3 if 3°C below for 10 min.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Zone temperature 2°C (3°F) above cooling setpoint for 10 minutes (Level 4) or 3°C (5°F) above for 10 minutes (Level 3). Similarly for low temperature relative to heating setpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: High-temperature alarm 1. If the zone is 2°C (3°F) above cooling setpoint for 10 minutes, generate a Level 4 alarm. 2. If the zone is 3°C (5°F) above cooling setpoint for 10 minutes, generate a Level 3 alarm. b. Low-temperature alarm 1. If the zone is 2°C (3°F) below heating setpoint for 10 minutes, generate a Level 4 alarm. 2. If the zone is 3°C (5°F) below heating setpoint for 10 minutes, generate a Level 3 alarm.

structured_payload_candidate.title: TAB – CHW-DPmax Determination
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Chilled Water Plant with DP controlled variable speed pumps
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Establish CHW-DPmax by fully opening downstream control valves, closing upstream valves, starting pumps, and adjusting speed until design flow is achieved through all open coils. Record DP sensor reading.
structured_payload_candidate.commissioning_check: Verify CHW-DPmax is established per TAB: fully open downstream valves, close upstream valves, start pumps, adjust speed until design flow achieved through open coils, record DP sensor reading.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Instructions for establishing CHW-DPmax should be provided in the Test and Balance Specification. For example: 1. Fully open all control valves serving coils that are located downstream of the differential pressure sensor. 2. Close the minimum flow bypass valve (if applicable). 3. Fully close all control valves serving coils that are located upstream of the differential pressure sensor. 4. Start pump(s). Manually adjust speed slowly until design flow (or design pressure drop, for coils without calibrated balance valves) is just achieved through all open coils without modulating any balance valves. One coil should be just at design flow, while others should be at or above design flow. 5. Once flow condition in previous step is achieved, note the BAS differential pressure sensor and handheld digital pressure sensor readings to verify accuracy of BAS reading; report BAS reading to controls contractor.

evidence_quote: 3.2.3.1.  CHW -DPmax, the maximum chilled water differential pressure setpoint, in psi
anchor_repair_reason: evidence_quote replaced with verbatim section heading line
evidence_text: 3.2.3.1.  CHW -DPmax, the maximum chilled water differential pressure setpoint, in psi
rejection_reason: weak evidence_quote: commissioning evidence lacks procedure/check action

structured_payload_candidate.title: TAB – Minimum Condenser Water Pump Speed (Variable Speed)
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Water-cooled chiller plant with variable speed CW pumps
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Establish MinCWPspeed by configuring plant for lowest chiller stage, reducing CW pump speed until flow through chiller condenser drops to 110% of minimum flow switch setpoint. Record speed.
structured_payload_candidate.commissioning_check: Verify MinCWPspeed is established per TAB: configure lowest stage, reduce CW pump speed until flow is 110% of minimum, record speed.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: For variable speed pumps, instructions for establishing MinCWPspeed should be provided in the Test and Balance Specification. For example: 1. Configure the plant for the lowest stage of chiller operation. Open the CW isolation valve of the test chiller and enable a CW pump and the corresponding number of cooling towers for that stage. 2. Reduce CW pump speed of the test chiller in 2% increments until the flow rate through the chiller’s condenser decreases to 110% of the minimum condenser water flow switch setpoint as determined by condenser differential pressure. 3. Note the minimum speed setpoint and report to controls contractor.

structured_payload_candidate.title: TAB – Minimum Primary CHW Pump Speed (Variable Primary-Variable Secondary)
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Variable primary-variable secondary chiller plant
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Establish Ch-MinPriPumpSpdStage by opening all chiller isolation valves, starting all non-redundant primary pumps at 100%, decreasing speed until flow through any chiller drops to 110% of CHW-MinFlowX. Record speed.
structured_payload_candidate.commissioning_check: Verify Ch-MinPriPumpSpdStage is established per TAB: open all isolation valves, start pumps at 100%, reduce speed until flow is 110% of minimum, record speed.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Instructions for establishing Ch-MinPriPumpSpd should be provided in the Test and Balance Specification. For example: 1. Open isolation valves of all chillers. 2. Start all non-redundant primary pumps at 100% speed. 3. Decrease primary pump speed in 2% increments starting from 100% until flowrate through any chiller, as measured by either evaporator barrel differential pressure or a flow meter, decreases to 110% of CHW-MinFlowX. 4. Note the speed setpoint and report to controls contractor.

structured_payload_candidate.title: TAB – Condenser Water Pump Design Speed per Stage
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Water-cooled chiller plant with variable speed CW pumps
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Establish Cw-DesPumpSpdStage for each chiller stage by configuring plant to that stage, reducing CW pump speed until condenser water flow through any chiller drops to 100% of design. Record speed.
structured_payload_candidate.commissioning_check: Verify Cw-DesPumpSpdStage for each stage per TAB: configure stage, reduce pump speed until flow is 100% of design, record speed.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Instructions for determining Cw-DesPumpSpdStage for each Stage should be provided in the Test and Balance Specification. For example: 1. Configure the plant to the lowest stage of chiller operation. Open the CW isolation valve of the chiller (s) in the stage and enable the CW pump(s) to operate in the stage and the corresponding number of cooling towers for the stage. 2. Reduce CW pump speed in 2% increments until condenser water flow through any chiller with an open CW isolation valve drops to 100% of design as determined by condenser differential pressure. 3. Note the speed setpoint and report to controls contractor. 4. Repeat the preceding steps for each chiller stage.

structured_payload_candidate.title: TAB – Return Fan Discharge Static Pressure Setpoints
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Multiple-zone VAV AHU with return fan and direct building pressure control
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Establish RFDSPmin as the DP required to deliver design return air volume at minimum OA with supply fan at design airflow. RFDSPmax as DP required to exhaust enough air to maintain building static pressure at 12 Pa with supply fan at design airflow and 100% OA.
structured_payload_candidate.commissioning_check: Verify RFDSPmin and RFDSPmax are established per TAB: measure DP at specified conditions.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Return-Fan Discharge Static Pressure Setpoints. (For return-fan direct building pressure control, see Section 5.16.10.) a. RFDSPmin. That required to deliver the design return air volume across the return air damper when the supply air fan is at design airflow and on minimum outdoor air. This setpoint shall be no less than 2.4 Pa (0.01 in. of water) to ensure outdoor air is not drawn backwards through the relief damper. b. RFDSPmax. That required to exhaust enough air to maintain building static pressure at setpoint 12 Pa (0.05 in. of water) when the supply air fan is at design airflow and on 100% outdoor air.

structured_payload_candidate.title: TAB – Single-Zone AHU Fan Speed Setpoints
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Single-zone VAV AHU
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Establish MinSpeed (supply airflow = DesOA with economizer fully open), MaxHeatSpeed (design heating airflow, default half of MaxCoolSpeed), and MaxCoolSpeed (design cooling airflow).
structured_payload_candidate.commissioning_check: Verify MinSpeed, MaxHeatSpeed, and MaxCoolSpeed per TAB: measure airflow at specified conditions.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Fan Speed Setpoints a. MinSpeed. The speed that provides supply airflow equal to DesOA (see Section3.1.6.2) with the economizer outdoor air damper fully open. b. MaxHeatSpeed. The speed that provides supply airflow equal to the design heating airflow scheduled on plans. If no heating airflow is provided on plans, default to half of the maximum cooling speed. c. MaxCoolSpeed. The speed that provides supply airflow equal to the design cooling airflow scheduled on plans.

structured_payload_candidate.title: TAB – Single-Zone AHU Return Fan Discharge Static Pressure Setpoints
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Single-zone VAV AHU with return fan and direct building pressure control
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Establish RFDSPmin (DP for design return air volume at minimum OA) and RFDSPmax (DP to maintain 12 Pa building pressure at 100% OA).
structured_payload_candidate.commissioning_check: Verify RFDSPmin and RFDSPmax per TAB: measure DP at specified conditions.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Return fan discharge static pressure setpoints (for Return Fan Direct Building Pressure Control, see Section 5.18.10.2). a. RFDSPmin: That required to deliver the design return air volume across the return air damper when the supply air fan is at design airflow and on minimum outdoor air. This setpoint shall be no less than 2.4 Pa (0.01 inches) to ensure outdoor air is not drawn backwards thru the relief damper. b. RFDSPmax: That required to exhaust enough air to maintain building static pressure at setpoint 12 Pa (0.05 inches) when the supply air fan is at design airflow and on 100% outdoor air.

structured_payload_candidate.title: TAB – FCU Fan Speed Setpoints
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Fan coil unit
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Establish MinHeatSpeed, MinCoolSpeed, DeadbandSpeed, MaxHeatSpeed, and MaxCoolSpeed per design or defaults.
structured_payload_candidate.commissioning_check: Verify fan speed setpoints per TAB: measure airflow at specified conditions or use defaults.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. MinHeatSpeed. The speed that provides supply airflow equal to the design heating minimum airflow scheduled on plans. If no minimum airflow is provided on plans, default to 20% of the maximum heating speed. b. MinCoolSpeed. The speed that provides supply airflow equal to the design cooling minimum airflow scheduled on plans. If no minimum airflow is provided on plans, default to 20% of the maximum cooling speed. c. DeadbandSpeed. If the fan is desired to operate when the zone is in deadband, set this value to less than or equal to MinSpeed. If the fan is to shut off when the zone is in deadband, set this value to 0. d. MaxHeatSpeed. The speed that provides supply airflow equal to the design heating airflow scheduled on plans. If no heating airflow is provided on plans, default to half of the maximum cooling speed. e. MaxCoolSpeed. The speed that provides supply airflow equal to the design cooling airflow scheduled on plans.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Zone Group Assignment Rules
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Multiple-zone VAV systems
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Each zone served by a single-zone air handler is its own Zone Group. Rooms occupied 24/7 are assigned to a single Zone Group. A Zone Group shall not span floors, exceed 2,300 m2, or include spaces from more than one tenant if future occupancy patterns are known. A zone shall not be in more than one Zone Group.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: 1. Each zone served by a single-zone air handler shall be its own Zone Group. 2. Rooms occupied 24/7, such as computer rooms, networking closets, mechanical, and electrical rooms served by the air handler shall be assigned to a single Zone Group. These rooms do not apply to the Zone Group restrictions below. 3. A Zone Group shall not span floors (per Section 6.4.3.3.4 of ASHRAE 90.1 2016). 4. A Zone Group shall not exceed 2,300 m2 (25,000 ft2) (per Section 6.4.3.3.4 of ASHRAE 90.1 2016). 5. If future occupancy patterns are known, a single Zone Group shall not include spaces belonging to more than one tenant. 6. A zone shall not be a member of more than one Zone Group.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Trim & Respond Logic Description
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Systems with setpoint reset (e.g., static pressure, temperature)
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Trim & Respond logic reduces a setpoint at a fixed rate until a downstream zone generates a request. When sufficient requests are present, the setpoint is increased. Requests are scaled by Importance-Multiplier. Request-hours are accumulated to identify rogue zones.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Trim & Respond (T&R) logic resets a setpoint for pressure, temperature, or other variables at an air handler or plant. It reduces the setpoint at a fixed rate until a downstream zone is no longer satisfied and generates a request. When a sufficient number of requests are present, the setpoint is increased in response. The importance of each zone’s requests can be adjusted to ensure that critical zones are always satisfied. When a sufficient number of requests no longer exist, the setpoint resumes decreasing at its fixed rate. A running total of the requests generated by each zone is kept to identify zones that are driving the reset logic.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Time-Averaged Ventilation (TAV) Logic
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: VAV terminal units
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When active airflow setpoint Vspt is nonzero and less than Vm (lowest controllable minimum), pulse width modulate: TAVratio = Vspt/Vm, total cycle time TCT = 15 min, open period OP = max(1.5 min, TCT * TAVratio), closed period CP = TCT - OP. During closed period, damper control loop is disabled. Randomize start times to avoid synchronized cycling.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: When the active airflow setpoint Vspt is nonzero and is less than the lowest possible airflow setpoint allowed by the controls (Vm), the airflow setpoint shall be pulse width modulated as follows: a. The time-averaged ventilation (TAV) ratio shall be determined as TAVratio = Vspt/Vm b. The total cycle time (TCT) shall be 15 minutes (adjustable) c. Open period. During the open period, the TAV airflow setpoint Vspt * shall be equal to Vm for a period of time OP, which is the larger of the following: d. 1.5 minutes or e. TCT multiplied by TAVratio f. Closed period. During the closed period, Vspt* shall be set to 0 for a period of time CP, where CP = TCT – OP. The VAV damper control loop shall be disabled with output set equal to 0 during the closed period. At the end of each closed period, the VAV damper shall be commanded to the last position from the previous open period prior to reenabling the control loop.

evidence_quote: 64 ASHRAE Guideline 36 -2021  5.2.2.  Time -Averaged Ventilation
